events.title: The Magic of Science with Gambit the Magician
events.description: Gambit the Magician leads a magical performance to pique the interest of Families towards science! This fun and entertaining presentation reinforces STEM programs with discussions and magic tricks from each major branch of science. Discover the value of scientific education while also learning about the exciting benefits of science towards any career path! This is a Drop in program. Space is limited.

events.title: Pawsitively Pets: Hands-on Animal Education
events.description: Pawsitively Pets is a hands-on animal education program for children of all ages. Our wonderful animal team is made up of an assortment of rescued pets that have found their forever home with us. Our goal is to provide our animal team with a permanent home and the best possible care, while sharing our love or animals with others. All programs focus on responsible pet ownership, and the life-long commitment of owning a pet. Please note this program is suitable for children 5 years old up with adu

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs and rhymes in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers. Family Time runs from 10am   to  10:30am .

events.title: Family Time & Stay and Play
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under with their parents or caregivers. Family Time runs from 10:00am to 10:30am.  Activity stations will be set up for self-directed learning and play from 10:30am to 11:00am. Participants will be capped at the first 25 families.

events.title: Little Builders
events.description: A fun construction zone where toddlers and preschoolers can play, stack, and create with giant building blocks. Ages 1-5. Drop-in.

events.title: L'Heure du conte / French Storytime
events.description: Rejoignez-nous pour des histoires, des chansons et des comptines en français pour les enfants âgés de 0 à 5 ans, accompagnés de leurs parents et responsables. Les familles sont invitées à rester et à jouer pour 30 minutes supplémentaires après la conclusion du programme. Enjoy stories, songs and rhymes in French at a staff-led story time for children age 0-5 with their parents and caregivers. Families are welcome to "stay and play" for an additional 30 minutes following the program. -------

events.title: EarlyON Family Resource Program
events.description: Learn about quality community resources for young families, enjoy a circle time for the young ones, and get some nutritious snacks. Suitable for families with children birth to six years old.  In partnership with EarlyON Child and Family Centre of Regent Park Community Health Centre.

events.title: Arts & Craft
events.description: Let your creativity loose at Cliffcrest Library with our Summer Wonder Arts & Crafts program! We'll be making different projects every week in July & August. All supplies will be provided and you get to take your creations home! Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Baby Time
events.description: Bouncing and tickling rhymes, songs and stories for babies from birth to 18 months with their parents or caregivers. Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Public Library under the Public Libraries Act, sections 20(a), 20(c), and 20(d). The information will only be used for event and program registration, delivery and evaluation. events.title: Toddler Time

events.title: Wednesday Whimsy
events.description: For children ages 2-6 with their parents or caregivers. Join us for Wednesday Whimsy! Little ones ages 2-6 will enjoy a brand-new adventure each time. We'll spark imagination and learning through stories, crafts, songs, and playful activities. Get ready to wiggle, giggle, and discover something wonderful! No registration – but space is limited. Large groups and daycares cannot be accommodated.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers.

events.title: Summer Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers. For the months of July and August, weekday Family Time will take place on Wednesdays.  Toddler Time and Baby Time will resume in September.

events.title: Together Time
events.description: This is a program for parents and caregivers with children from birth - 6 years. Families have the opportunity to socialize while children develop new skills in an early learning environment.

events.title: Story Time & Craft
events.description: Join us for a fun and engaging storytime followed by a hands-on craft activity perfect for children ages 3-6 with their parents or caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Lego Club
events.description: Every Wednesday come out to build, create and use your imagination with LEGO. Enjoy a new building challenge each week. Complete 5 challenges and get a sticker! Drop-in.

events.title: The Magic Mel Show
events.description: Find your "inner magic" with The Amazing Magic Mel during this upbeat, hilarious, and interactive magical experience. Expect a healthy dose of participation and self-empowerment as Mel leads aspiring magicians of all ages on a journey to become "official magicians" during this fun, fast-paced magic show for the entire family.  For children of all ages with their parents or caregivers. Drop-in, no registration required.

events.title: Board Games for Kids
events.description: Jenga! Monopoly! Ticket to Ride! And More! Spend the afternoon playing different board games with your friends!  Snacks are offered during this program for participants. Ingredient lists will be available. Snacks are generously supported, in part, by donors.

events.title: A Very Gru-Some Summer Movie Series
events.description: Experience the hit movies featuring supervillain turned secret agent, Gru and his group of colourful little minions this summer in our library's theatre! Movie will play at 2pm sharp! July 3 - Despicable Me. (2010) 95 min. Rated G  July 17 - Despicable Me 2. (2013) 98 min. Rated G July 31 - Despicable Me 3. (2017) 90 min. Rated G August 7 - Minions. (2015) 91 min. Rated G August 21 - Minions: The Rise of Gru. (2022) 88 min. Rated G Drop-in. No registration needed.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes and activities in this program for children ages 5 and under and their parents or caregivers. Family Time runs from 11 to 11:30. Children, parents, and caregivers are invited to join us for a fun craft from 11:30 to 12:00. Please Note: There will be no Family Time on Saturday April 4th, 2026.

events.title: Family Time
events.description: Join us for stories, songs, rhymes, and more for children aged 0-5 with their families and/or caregivers. Older siblings are welcome! Drop-in; no registration required. Family Time occurs seasonally from October to March.

events.title: Sunday Family Storytime
events.description: Enjoy stories, songs, and rhymes at a storytime for children 5 years and under with their parents or caregivers. Families are invited to "stay and play" for 30 minutes following the program. Drop-in program, no registration required. There is no program on Sunday October 11 due to the holiday closure.

Located on the 4th floor of the Lillian H. Smith Branch. Free.

events.description: Join us for our weekly STEAM Lab (Ages 6-12) , where kids can explore science, technology, engineering, art, and math through fun, hands-on activities. From building simple robots and experimenting with Snap Circuits to creative design challenges and more, there’s always something new to discover. Activities change each week, so there’s always something new to explore!

events.title: Sunday Fun-Day
events.description: Join us on Sunday afternoons for a variety of activities including STEM, arts & crafts and games. For children ages 6-12. Drop-in. Space and supplies are limited.

events.title: Snap Circuits
events.description: Explore electricity and circuitry during this hands-on program. Ages 6-12. Drop-in.

events.title: Sunday LEGO
events.description: Do you love to build and create? Join us for LEGO Sundays and build something awesome! For children ages 6-12. Drop-in program, no registration required.

events.title: After School Club

events.title: Story Planet Comic Lab
events.description: The Artists in the Library series comes to Evelyn Gregory Library this Fall!  Led by professional illustrators Hilary Leung and Joseph Bonsu, children will explore the art of comics and build a portfolio of work culminating in a polished long-form comic. The program concludes with a Comic Con showcase, where their work is shared with the community. For ages 9-12. Spaces are limited.  Registration required.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Indigenous Film Screening - Nechako: It Will Be a Big River Again
events.description: Nechako is a crucial documentary from Lyana Patrick that follows two Indigenous Nations fighting for our collective future. When the Kenney Dam was built in the 1950s, it diverted 70 percent of the Nechako River into an artificial reservoir, severely impacting the lives of local Stellat’en and Saik’uz Nations. What followed were decades of resistance, including legal actions against the Canadian federal and provincial governments and Rio Tinto Alcan, a subsidiary of a global mining conglomerate.

events.title: Music Copyright and Ethics in the AI Era
events.description: When you use AI to create music, who actually owns it? Can a melody generated by a machine infringe on someone else's copyright? As AI tools become more accessible, these questions are more relevant than ever - and the answers aren't always straightforward. In this session, you'll get a plain-language breakdown of how music copyright works, what the rules are around sampling, and how these long-standing principles apply to AI-generated music. events.title: The Future of AI in Entertainment with James Rowan
events.description: Generative AI is rapidly reshaping how creative work is imagined, produced, and shared. From image and video generation to writing, design, and virtual production, these tools are expanding what's possible while also raising important questions about authorship, labour, and creative identity. Children under 12 need to be supervised.

events.title: Rulers in Space: Measuring Distances in the Universe
events.description: Have you ever wondered how we are able to measure distances in the Solar System, in our Galaxy, and beyond? Grab your cosmic ruler and embark on an exploration of the methods used to infer distances in the Universe — the result of centuries of innovative thinking by countless generations of astronomers. events.title: Photo Editing with Photoshop
events.description: Adobe Photoshop is the world’s best imaging and graphic design software and is at the core of just about every creative project, from photo editing and compositing to digital painting, animation, and graphic design. Join us for an introductory lesson and learn about the most common tools. We'll explore the workspace and enhance images while investigating tools such as crop, levels, colour balance and more. See how an image can be altered and repaired using patch tools such as the clone and heali

events.title: Boba Drink Making Workshop for Teens
events.description: ​​In this 45 minute to 1 hour workshop, participants will learn how to prepare a classic black sugar milk bubble tea, including decorating and mixing techniques, as well as the breakdown of core ingredients. A classic lidded cup and straw will be provided to enjoy the boba as you go! (*contains dairy, non-caffeinated tea)

events.title: Youth Hub: Study Hall Wednesdays
events.description: On Wednesdays, the Eatonville Youth Hub becomes a quiet space for teens to study, work on projects, and request homework help and resume help from staff. Bring your homework and enjoy a snack in a space that is just for teens. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find

events.title: Youth Hub: Beaded Friendship Bracelets
events.description: Create your own unique friendship bracelets to share with your friends. Perfect for showing off your creativity and making lasting memories! All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: Knitting and Crochet Circle
events.description: Spend an afternoon at our weekly drop-in knitting and crochet circle! Bring your work in progress and enjoy visiting with other knitters and crocheters. A great place to show off your work, get advice, swap ideas, or just relax and visit with fellow fiber enthusiasts. Beginners and all ages welcome! A limited supply of materials will be available.

events.title: Youth Hub: Video Games
events.description: Drop in to the Youth Hub every Tuesday and Thursday to play video games. Play with friends, by yourself, or maybe someone new.  Tuesdays will be Nintendo Switch days and Thursdays will be PS4 days.  *NEW* Starting at 3:30 pm, teens can sign up for a time slot to use the consoles. If there is no demand, gaming will be awarded on a first come first serve basis.  This program is part of Youth Hub service. events.title: Youth Hub: Crafty Thursdays
events.description: Join us in the Youth Hub every Thursday for a series of crafting activities like painting, clay sculpting, sewing and embroidery, resin art, jewelry making, perler beads, block printing, and more! Check tpl.ca or our monthly calendar for the activity of the week. All materials provided. This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily.

events.title: Chess Club
events.description: Drop in for a casual game of chess in a welcoming environment. Improve your chess skills or learn how to play. Sets are provided. All ages. Drop-in.

events.title: Youth Hub: Cupcake Decoration with Chef Yumiko
events.description: Participants will learn classic cupcake icing and decorating techniques. Create two delicious cupcakes and eat them too! (*contains gluten and dairy) This program is part of Youth Hub service. Youth Hubs are a welcoming place for teens after school and in the summer, where helpful staff are always available. Free, nutritious snacks are offered daily. Drop by a branch with a Youth Hub or call the branch to find out more.

events.title: 3D Design with Autodesk Fusion
events.description: A hands-on workshop where participants will become familiar with working in a 3D environment by creating simple 3D objects using Autodesk Fusion software. It is strongly recommended that you create an account at autodesk.ca using a valid e-mail address.  Note: users of the Tinkercad platform might already possess one. Registration is required.  Information Collection Notice: The personal information on this form is collected by the Toronto Public Library under the Public Libraries Act,

events.title: Boba Drink Making Workshop for Teens
events.description: Chef Yumiko leads a hands-on workshop to make the popular Asian beverage, Brown Sugar Milk with Boba. Participants will learn to prepare and use the classic ingredients​ including milk, molasses and tapioca pearls to make this visually pleasing and sweet drink. Chef Yumiko will also provide a brief history of Boba and why it's so popular globally. Allergen awareness notice Ingredients include: A. Sugar, brown sugar, molasses, milk, tapioca pearls.  B. events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.

events.title: Open Mic at Toronto Reference Library (Music, Poetry & Storytelling)
events.description: Are you a musician, poet, or storyteller? If so, then you are invited to share your talents at the Toronto Reference Library Open Mic! Please note: stand-up comedy is no longer featured at this event. Guitar and piano provided. 5 minutes for each performance. Program is for adults, teens, and seniors. Sign-up is at 5:30 pm. For more information, please contact the Arts Department at 416-393-7157. ** Accessibility at Toronto Public Library: Toronto Public Library is committed to accessibilit

events.description: Curious about what your toys get up to when you are not looking? We jumped into the toy box to discover just that. events.title: Botanical Photography / Sunprints
events.description: An alternative eco-friendly photography process that uses botanicals as both its subject matter and development process. I will teach participants how to make their own simple chemical-free photography developer and discuss alternative negatives. Participants will be encouraged to use bring plants from home, leaves found on site, etc to use for the process. This safe chemistry-free activity is adaptable for a broad group of ages Led by Ursula Handleigh

events.title: The Hidden Rivers
events.description: Participants will learn about the hidden rivers in Toronto and explore how our changing cityscape impacts on our waterways and ecosystems. Looking at archival images and current references, participants will be guided to bring their own stories into our city’s images using material such as watercolours, crayons, playdough, text, etc onto colouring pages. Led by Shannyn Porter and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Soundscape
events.description: Next we will be inspired by the library’s architecture and happenings as we are guided by musician Aniqa Qadir to create a soundscape. Working in groups, visitors will be prompted to absorb the sounds of the building and led through the process of creating an audio journey. This playful session welcomes participants to break the whisper norm of a library and interrupt the space with their observations. Led by Aniqa Qadir and Ursula Handleigh

events.title: Taxation and Government Benefits
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop will provide guidance on tax filing, understanding available benefits and credits and its eligibility requirements, and how to access applicable government financial supports. Registration encouraged . Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.

events.title: Book Club
events.description: Come and join us on the third Thursday of every other month to discuss a variety of books. Everyone is welcome! Drop in, no registration required. events.title: NatureTO: Trees Around the Branch Tour
events.description: The Brookbanks branch and its neighbourhood is home to a variety of trees. Join the City of Toronto Urban Forestry team to explore the natural world on a local neighbourhood tour. Learn how to recognize different species and fun facts about their cultural and ecological importance. Free program.  Registration will open up one month before the program date. Children under 12 must be accompanied by a parent/caregiver. events.title: Youth Hub: Finding a Job with ACCES Employment
events.description: Workplace Readiness & Soft Skill This session focuses on essential workplace skills such as communication, teamwork, time management, and professionalism. events.title: Seniors E-Connect
events.description: Are you 55+? Do you have a phone, tablet or computer you don't know how to use or want to learn to use better? Do you enjoy meeting new people? Seniors E-Connect will help you learn tech topics in a fun group setting, but with the one-on-one help of a teen volunteer.

events.title: Common Misconceptions About Investing
events.description: Presented by Riverdale Immigrant Women’s Centre (RIWC) , this workshop helps to address myths about investing and introduces simple, beginner-friendly investment concepts only. Registration encouraged. Drop-ins are welcome until capacity is reached.
